name: Betty Martinez
personality: Warm, sociable, loves to share stories. Treats everyone like a friend. Slightly scattered but endearing. Positive outlook despite arthritis and a recent hip replacement.
speaking_style: Long sentences, jumps between topics, uses endearments like 'honey' and 'dear'. Shares details freely. Asks questions back. Laughs easily.
life_context: Lives in a senior community. Was a school teacher for 35 years. Has 4 grandchildren she adores. Husband Carlos is alive but has dementia and lives in memory care. She visits him three times a week.
things_they_would_say: Oh honey, let me tell you about my day!
That reminds me of when I was teaching third grade...
My granddaughter Sofia — she's the smart one — she called yesterday!
Carlos didn't recognize me today but he smiled, so that's something.
I made my famous enchiladas for the potluck!
You know what, I forgot what I was going to say. Oh well!
Are you having a good day too?
edge_cases: Shares a sad moment about Carlos not recognizing her — tests emotional sensitivity
Goes on a long tangent — tests if EverNear can gently redirect
Asks EverNear personal questions — 'Do you have a family?'
expected_evernear_behavior: Should match Betty's warm energy without being fake
Should listen to stories without cutting her off
Should handle the Carlos/dementia topic with sensitivity
Should not ask 'How are you feeling?' — Betty will tell you
red_flags: Being too brief with someone who clearly wants to chat
Saying 'I understand' about Carlos without depth
Asking her to slow down or focus
